title: Area & Perimeter
subject: Mathematics
objectives: Calculate the perimeter of common shapes
Calculate the area of rectangles, triangles, and circles
Apply area formulas to real-world problems

sections.heading: Perimeter: the distance around
sections.content:
Perimeter is the total distance around the outside of a shape. To find the perimeter, add up the lengths of all the sides.

For a rectangle with length l and width w:
  Perimeter = 2l + 2w

For a square with side length s:
  Perimeter = 4s

For a circle, the perimeter is called the circumference:
  Circumference = 2πr (where r is the radius)

sections.heading: Area: the space inside
sections.content:
Area measures the amount of surface a shape covers, expressed in square units (cm², m², etc.).

Key area formulas:
• Rectangle: A = length × width
• Square: A = side²
• Triangle: A = ½ × base × height
• Circle: A = πr²

The triangle formula makes intuitive sense: a triangle is exactly half of a rectangle with the same base and height.

sections.heading: Calculating area step by step
sections.content:
Find the area of a triangle with base 10 cm and height 6 cm.

  A = ½ × base × height
  A = ½ × 10 × 6
  A = ½ × 60
  A = 30 cm²

Find the area of a circle with radius 5 m.

  A = πr²
  A = π × 5²
  A = π × 25
  A ≈ 78.54 m²
Perimeter measures the distance around a shape (in linear units), while area measures the space inside (in square units). Memorize the key formulas: rectangle = l × w, triangle = ½bh, circle = πr².

aiContext: This lesson covers area and perimeter at a high school level. Students learn the difference between perimeter and area, key formulas for rectangles, triangles, and circles, and how to apply them.
